skill.name: cc-devflow-orchestrator
skill.description: Routes CC-DevFlow users to the right workflow command, phase gate, or specialist agent for requirements, planning, development, review, QA, and release work.
skill.summary: Routes CC-DevFlow phases to the correct commands, gates, and specialist agents.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium alerts. Manual review found Markdown command examples, local file references, and workflow words, not executable code or reconnaissance logic. The skill recommends local commands, so users should trust the CC-DevFlow project files before following them.

low_findings.title: Local Command And Script Recommendations
low_findings.description: The skill recommends CC-DevFlow slash commands and local helper scripts, including prerequisite, status, and constitution validation scripts. The Markdown file does not execute these commands, but users should review the referenced project scripts before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review confirms the commands are documentation recommendations, not executable code in the skill. The caution remains valid because users may later execute local scripts from their project.

content.value_statement: Complex CC-DevFlow projects can stall when users do not know the next command or agent. This skill maps each phase to the correct workflow command, gate, and specialist handoff.
actual_capabilities: Maps project-level setup to roadmap, architecture, guidelines, and style commands.
Routes requirement phases from initialization through PRD, design, tasks, development, review, QA, and release.
Recommends specialist agents for PRD writing, technical design, UI design, planning, QA, and review.
Summarizes entry and exit gates for CC-DevFlow phases.
Provides troubleshooting routes for blocked gates, missing documents, constitution checks, and TDD order issues.
Lists auxiliary commands for status checks, task updates, workflow restarts, upgrades, verification, and bug fixes.
limitations: It is a routing reference and does not implement the detailed standards used by each agent or command.
It depends on the surrounding CC-DevFlow commands, agents, scripts, and documentation being present in the project.
It does not validate project state by itself or execute phase gates automatically.
Some workflow labels and notes are bilingual, so teams may need shared terminology.

use_cases.title: Start a New Requirement
use_cases.description: Find the correct sequence from research initialization to clarification, PRD creation, and quality checklist review.

use_cases.title: Move From Planning to Delivery
use_cases.description: Route a completed PRD into technical design, UI prototype work, epic planning, and TDD execution.

use_cases.title: Recover a Blocked Workflow
use_cases.description: Identify the current phase, inspect gate requirements, and choose the next command for review, QA, release, or verification.

prompt_templates.title: Find My Next Step
prompt_templates.prompt: I am working in CC-DevFlow and my requirement is initialized. Which command should I run next, and what should it produce?

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: I need help with a PRD, technical design, UI prototype, or QA review. Route me to the right CC-DevFlow command and agent.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Gate Blocker
prompt_templates.prompt: My CC-DevFlow phase gate is blocked. Use the orchestrator to identify likely missing files, checks, and next commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an End-to-End Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an end-to-end CC-DevFlow route for a requirement from research through release, including optional checks and review gates.

output_examples.input: The requirement has research and brainstorming completed, but no PRD yet.
output_examples.output: Recommended next step: run the PRD workflow. Expected output is a completed PRD aligned with the brainstorming document.

output_examples.input: Development is complete and the team wants quality checks before QA.
output_examples.output: Recommended next step: run the two-stage review workflow, then proceed to QA after both review reports pass.

output_examples.input: A developer reports a login timeout bug and wants to start fixing it.
output_examples.output: Recommended route: use the bug-fix workflow so the team investigates root cause, patterns, hypotheses, and a test-driven fix.

best_practices: Use the current orchestration status before choosing a command.
Run optional clarification, checklist, and UI steps when ambiguity or user experience risk is high.
Review local scripts and project rules before executing recommended helper commands.
anti_patterns: Do not skip PRD alignment when brainstorming is the requirement source of truth.
Do not duplicate detailed standards in the orchestrator when specialist agents own them.
Do not start implementation before planning artifacts and TDD task order are ready.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes CC-DevFlow users to the right command, phase gate, or specialist agent for the current workflow state.

faq.question: Does it execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown routing guide that recommends commands and scripts for the user or assistant to consider.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it replace the detailed CC-DevFlow agents?
faq.answer: No. It points to specialist agents and command files, but it does not contain their full execution standards.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when starting a requirement, choosing the next phase, recovering from a blocked gate, or routing review and QA work.

faq.question: What should I check before running recommended scripts?
faq.answer: Review the local project scripts and rules they reference, because this skill only documents the route.
